muscles.name: Chest
muscles.anatomical_location: Anterior thorax between ribs and sternum
muscles.description: The pectoralis major and minor muscles form the chest. They are responsible for horizontal adduction and internal rotation of the humerus.

muscles.name: Shoulders
muscles.description: The deltoid muscles form the shoulder cap. They are responsible for abduction, flexion, and extension of the humerus.

muscles.name: Biceps
muscles.description: The biceps brachii is a two-headed muscle on the anterior upper arm. It is responsible for elbow flexion and forearm supination.

muscles.name: Triceps
muscles.description: The triceps brachii is a three-headed muscle on the posterior upper arm. It is responsible for elbow extension.

muscles.name: Forearms
muscles.description: The forearm muscles including flexors and extensors. They are responsible for wrist and finger movements.

muscles.name: Upper Back
muscles.description: The trapezius muscles form the upper back. They are responsible for shoulder elevation, retraction, and depression.

muscles.name: Lower Back
muscles.description: The erector spinae and other lower back muscles. They are responsible for spinal extension and support.

muscles.name: Lats
muscles.description: The latissimus dorsi muscles form the lats. They are responsible for shoulder adduction, extension, and internal rotation.

muscles.name: Upper Abs
muscles.description: The upper portion of the rectus abdominis. Primarily responsible for spinal flexion in the upper abdomen.

muscles.name: Lower Abs
muscles.description: The lower portion of the rectus abdominis and transversus abdominis. Responsible for lower spinal flexion and pelvic control.

muscles.name: Obliques
muscles.description: The external and internal oblique muscles. They are responsible for trunk rotation and lateral flexion.

muscles.name: Gluteus Maximus
muscles.description: The largest of the gluteal muscles. Responsible for hip extension and external rotation.

muscles.name: Gluteus Medius
muscles.description: A medium-sized gluteal muscle. Responsible for hip abduction and stabilization.

muscles.name: Gluteus Minimus
muscles.description: The smallest of the gluteal muscles. Assists with hip abduction and stabilization.

muscles.name: Quadriceps
muscles.description: The quadriceps femoris consists of four muscles on the anterior thigh. They are responsible for knee extension and hip flexion.

muscles.name: Hamstrings
muscles.description: The hamstring group consists of three muscles on the posterior thigh. They are responsible for knee flexion and hip extension.

muscles.name: Hip Flexors
muscles.description: Muscles responsible for hip flexion, including the sartorius and iliopsoas. Essential for lifting the leg and running.

muscles.name: Calves
muscles.description: The calf muscles including gastrocnemius and soleus. They are responsible for ankle plantarflexion.
